comments_clusters.representative_excerpt: The proposed rule fundamentally changes how reduction-in-force (RIF) retention standing is determined. Under the current system, tenure and length of service dominate, with performance ratings providing marginal adjustments through additional service credit. The proposal instead centers performance ratings as the primary ranking factor, relegating tenure and...

I oppose this proposal. This would place the greatest weight on an employee’s three most recent performance evaluations. However, there evaluations are likely to be reliable, since agency managers were instructed last year to avoid issuing top performance ratings and instead assign lower scores more broadly, regardless of actual job performance.
